name: Ballerina BIR
summary: The single shared IR between Ballerina's two compilation pipelines (jBallerina → JVM bytecode, nBallerina → LLVM IR → native). Cached as a linking artifact in `.ballerina/` for incremental builds. Forces target-neutral semantics — neither JVM-isms nor LLVM-isms can leak into BIR.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Ballerina IR (BIR) is the shared IR between Ballerina's two compilation pipelines: **jBallerina** (BIR → JVM bytecode) and **nBallerina** (BIR → LLVM IR → native binary). BIR is generated from the desugared AST after lowering high-level constructs. It is deliberately lower-level than the AST but higher-level than either target bytecode.

sources.label: Peering into the Ballerina Intermediate Representation

sections.title: Architectural details
sections.content:
Two architectural details stand out:

1. **BIR is cached as a linking artifact.** Each package's BIR is serialised to binary and cached in `.ballerina/`, letting incremental builds resolve cross-module references without re-parsing source. Cross-module type checking and dependency resolution work against cached BIR, similar to how Rust caches `.rmeta` files.

2. **The dual-target requirement forces cleanly target-neutral semantics.** BIR cannot bake in JVM-isms or LLVM-isms because both backends consume it. Memory models, calling conventions, exception handling, concurrency primitives — all must be expressible without reference to any specific runtime. The constraint is real engineering work but produces a cleaner IR than either target alone would.

sections.title: Lesson — dual-target IR design
sections.content:
The compiler-side implication is that a shared mid-level artifact with per-target lowering is cleaner than duplicating the pipeline once a language needs multiple serious backends — but only if the IR avoids assumptions from any one backend.

The pattern generalises: a language wanting both managed-runtime (JVM, .NET) and native (LLVM, Cranelift) backends benefits from a single mid-level IR designed deliberately target-neutrally. Retrofitting target-neutrality after one backend has been built is much harder than designing it in.

sources.label: JVM Compiler Backend for Ballerina IR (dissertation)
